test.note: Current HEAD passes full Vitest suite: 39 test files, 414 tests.

lint.note: Project services manifest defines lint as a no-op because no lint tool is configured.

reviewsSummary.note: Round 3 re-reviewed compatibility-hardening against current mission metadata and current HEAD. Three legacy review artifacts now fail due to stale feature/commit attribution drift; ten current-head reviews pass.

blockingIssues.description: Legacy scrutiny artifact references a feature ID absent from mission features/transcripts and an unreachable commit, so the feature cannot be authoritatively reviewed against current HEAD.
Existing review artifact is stale: it claims broader aggregate/freshness hardening than the cited commit implements and contains outdated claims about current 400 validation envelopes.
Existing review artifact is stale: it asserts null history market_data and single-point market_chart behavior that no longer match current HEAD tests or implementation.

featureReviews.summary: Round 3 review found the feature ID no longer exists in mission metadata or transcript records, and its prior review references an unreachable commit.
Round 3 review found the existing pass report overstated the cited commit scope and contains stale claims about current validation envelopes.
Round 3 review found the existing pass report no longer matches current HEAD behavior for coin history and market chart responses.
Round 3 review remapped the stale report to the current cross-area integration evidence and found no code issues.
Round 3 review confirmed the seeded detail-fixture hardening still holds on current HEAD.
Round 3 review confirmed the exchange-rate contract characterization remains accurate on current HEAD.
Existing Round 2 review still matches current HEAD and no blocking issues were found.
Existing Round 2 review still matches current HEAD and no blocking issues were found.
Existing Round 2 review still matches current HEAD and no blocking issues were found.
Existing Round 2 review still matches current HEAD and no blocking issues were found.
Round 3 review confirmed the audit document and normalized 400 envelope coverage still match current HEAD.
Round 3 review confirmed the parity gate documentation and version bump remain accurate on current HEAD.
Round 3 review confirmed the direct cross-area integration assertions remain isolated and correct on current HEAD.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how scrutiny should handle legacy review files whose feature IDs or commit references drift out of mission metadata/current HEAD, so reruns can distinguish true code regressions from stale artifact mismatches.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 3 found three compatibility-hardening review artifacts that no longer map to current mission features or current-head behavior, causing scrutiny failures even though validators are green.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether report-only parity-gate features must still perform manual curl checks or may rely on audit documents plus automated validators when no runtime code changes are made.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The parity-target-and-release-gate review found the worker followed audit/test/typecheck validation only, while the skill text still requires three representative curl checks.

rejectedObservations.observation: Consider adding a lint tool for the project.
rejectedObservations.reason: Out of scope for this scrutiny rerun and not required to validate the compatibility-hardening milestone.

notes: Round 3 revalidated current HEAD. Validators are green, but scrutiny now fails because three legacy compatibility-hardening review artifacts are stale or no longer map to mission metadata/current-head behavior. User-testing should wait until orchestrator resolves the feature/review attribution drift or creates explicit fix-up features.

override.justification: Round 3 validators are green and the remaining failures are stale legacy scrutiny artifacts that no longer map to current mission features or reachable current-head commits. Current compatibility-hardening features passed review, and mission AGENTS guidance now tells validators to escalate this artifact drift instead of treating it as a code regression.
